item.title: cmd_backlog_edit flushes pending AC entry without JSON encoding
item.intent: AC entries appended via --ac must be correctly JSON-encoded — unencoded special characters silently produce invalid JSON and corrupt the backlog.
item.description: In cmd_backlog_edit(), when a second --ac flag is encountered, the pending AC entry from the prior --ac is flushed to ac_entries with 'ac_entries+=("$pending_ac")' (line ~3898) — no JSON encoding. cmd_backlog_add() correctly uses 'ac_entries+=(""\"$pending_ac\""")' in the same flush path. If the AC text contains '"', backslash, or other JSON metacharacters, the constructed JSON will be invalid, silently corrupting the item's acceptanceCriteria. The final-flush path at line ~3942 IS correctly encoded — only the mid-loop flush is broken.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_edit() and locate the --ac case (around line 3894)
Find the flush block: 'if [[ -n "$pending_ac" ]]; then ac_entries+=("$pending_ac"); fi'
Replace the flush with the encoded form matching cmd_backlog_add: 'ac_entries+=("\"\"$pending_ac\""")'
Verify: 'backlog edit <ID> --ac 'has "quotes"' --ac 'second entry'' produces valid JSON in the backlog file
acceptanceCriteria: backlog edit <ID> --ac 'text with "quotes"' --ac 'second' stores both entries with proper JSON encoding
backlog edit with plain AC entries (no special chars) still works correctly after the fix
